tasks.subject: Hard audit of founding spec + master plan index + Plan 01
tasks.notes: Audit ran via dispatched general-purpose agent with risk-auditor persona inlined (Path A adapted). Returned RED with 28 findings + DESIGN-RETHINK FLAG. Report at 00_META/proposals/2026-05-14_audit-report.md. Walkthrough produced amendment plan at 00_META/proposals/2026-05-14_amendments.md. Major outcome: Plan 01 splits into 01a/01b/01c.

tasks.subject: Terrain prep — git identity, accounts, age key, origin remote, context-file rewrite
tasks.notes: Pre-Batch-1 readiness session per Ricardo's pivot ('clean the terrain before pouring foundation'). Journal at 00_META/journal/2026-05-14_terrain-prep.md.

All 28 audit findings + 6 recommendations either landed or scheduled. Journal at 00_META/journal/2026-05-14_batch-1-amendments.md.

tasks.subject: JP coordination items requested 2026-05-14 — awaiting reply
tasks.notes: JP replied 2026-05-15 (Signal + Proton email) to all 6 items: (1) Telegram chat_id 459242980 ✓; (2) age pubkey via Proton email — added to infra/age-recipients.txt 2026-05-15 (closes t-jp-age-keypair); (3) OS = macOS Sequoia 15.7.3 — NOT Linux Mint as recommended (closes t-jp-os-confirmation, opens t-macos-deviation-decision); (4) Gitea — JP has no account, asked why; Ricardo will create server-side and send password via Signal (tracked in t-ricardo-jp-onboarding-actions); (5) Notion — JP created workspace and invited contacto@nexostrat.com; Ricardo to accept (tracked in t-ricardo-jp-onboarding-actions); (6) GitHub — JP confirmed plan to create new account with contacto@nexostrat.com; Ricardo to create (tracked in t-ricardo-jp-onboarding-actions).

tasks.subject: JP age keypair generation — awaiting Signal reply
tasks.notes: JP generated age keypair on jp-mac (macOS Sequoia 15.7.3) and sent pubkey via Proton email 2026-05-15. Pubkey age10k4rzha64ykqtjrjkqut6dyzxy5qexu2038lpe6gqk58l7rx4p4qrupv79 added to infra/age-recipients.txt. Format validated by encrypting test string to both recipients (age accepted both -R parses; decrypt-side requires tty for Ricardo's passphrase, deferred to Plan 01a Task 13 bidirectional roundtrip during execution). tasks.subject: JP machine OS confirmation — awaiting Signal reply
tasks.notes: JP confirmed 2026-05-15: macOS Sequoia 15.7.3 (NOT Linux Mint as recommended). Triggers macOS-deviation work — see t-macos-deviation-decision. Concrete impacts: Plan 01a Task 6 jp-heavy.yaml currently declares os: linux-mint and needs patch; spec §3 secrets discipline (/dev/shm RAM-tmpfs + shred) is Linux-specific and needs macOS-equivalent (RAM disk via diskutil + hdiutil; shred via gshred from brew coreutils or RAM-disk unmount). age tooling itself works fine on macOS.

tasks.subject: Ricardo-side actions to complete JP onboarding (Gitea + Notion + GitHub)
tasks.notes: All three sub-actions resolved 2026-05-15 in different ways. (1) Gitea user creation: POSTPONED INDEFINITELY — JP-Light variant opts out of Gitea web (he wants results, not architecture browsing). His interface is Telegram + email + FOSS dashboard (Plan 02). User can be created in ~10 min server-side any time he asks. (2) Notion accept: CANCELLED per ADR-038 — Notion exits Nexostrat at firm level; the JP-personal Nexostrat workspace stays JP-personal. (3) GitHub account: ALREADY DONE during 2026-05-14 terrain prep (username `nexostrat`, email contacto@nexostrat.com, SSH key nexostrat_ed25519). Verified 2026-05-15 via ssh -T returning friendly greeting.

tasks.subject: Decide macOS-deviation path: quick triage patch vs full amendment cycle
tasks.notes: MOOT — JP picked Light mode 2026-05-15. Light JP never decrypts vault content locally except a one-time sentinel for Plan 01a Task 13 Direction B (innocuous content, plain /tmp is fine). The /dev/shm/shred discipline only matters for Heavy mode. jp-heavy.yaml in Plan 01a Task 6 remains as a future-state stub for whenever JP flips to Heavy; the macOS adaptation work attaches to that flip event, not to Stage 1. Plan 01a fully execute-ready as-is.

tasks.subject: Plan 02 brainstorm — pick FOSS self-hosted replacement(s) for Notion's four roles
tasks.notes: Per ADR-038. Four roles to cover: meeting capture canonical (Whisper.cpp/Jitsi/etc.), summary generation (Ollama/etc.), CRM (EspoCRM/SuiteCRM/Krayin/Twenty/Vikunja/custom), collaborative docs workspace (AppFlowy/Outline/BookStack/AFFiNE/Wiki.js/Logseq/Trilium/HedgeDoc/Gitea Wiki). All options open; Plan 02 brainstorm decides. May pick one tool covering multiple roles or specialized tools per role. JP's interface (Telegram + dashboard) means the dashboard choice is the load-bearing JP-facing decision in Plan 02.

tasks.subject: Plan 01a text amendments — fix plan-text drift surfaced during Tasks 1-11 execution
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-05-16 in commit 7e950ee as part of the post-hard-audit patch arc. All 11 process-substitution sites in 00_META/plans/2026-05-14_plan-01a-foundation.md simplified to direct -i form (10 tilde-form uses + 1 PRIV_KEY_AGE wrapper-text-in-plan), age 1.1.0+ semantics verified. Task 11 Step 3 wording fixed (head -1 AGE-SECRET-KEY-1 check → grep -q '^AGE-SECRET-KEY-1' anywhere — robust against the standard `# created` line-1 comment). Live wrapper infra/scripts/run-with-secrets.sh:53 patched in lockstep. Surfaced + fixed an unrelated 4th issue: Plan 01a's Task 3 documentation contained LITERAL sk-ant-test... fixture strings that the secret-scan hook (installed in Task 3 itself) now blocks on re-edit — patched by adopting the runtime-assembly trick the actual test_secret_scan_hook.sh already uses (PREFIX/SUFFIX/printf). Plan 01b + Plan 01c carry the same process-sub pattern; deferred to their respective re-audit cycles per hard-audit recommendation.

Companion task t-spec-cost-table-amendment FOLDED INTO this sweep (cost-table reality now matches across spec §5 + cost-sharing-agreement.md + PARTNERSHIP_AGREEMENT.md). ONE deferred sub-item: secrets.env.age re-encryption without NOTION_API_KEY= line — requires TTY for Ricardo's age passphrase, tracked in t-plan-01a-jp-and-tty-deferred (MANIFEST.md NOTION row marked DEPRECATED 2026-05-16 with note pointing to the TTY-deferred task).

tasks.notes: DONE 2026-05-16 via Plan 01b Task 1 commit 615372a. Confirmed path: /srv/gitea/data/git/repositories/nexostrat/nexostrat.git (owner ricardo:ricardo host-side, no chmod ceremony needed). tasks.subject: Batch 3 step 1 — Re-audit Plan 01a (independent risk-auditor pass)
tasks.notes: Dispatched 2026-05-14 via general-purpose agent with risk-auditor persona inlined (same pattern as 2026-05-14 founding-spec audit). Returned YELLOW (large): 0 CRITICAL, 7 HIGH, 5 MEDIUM, 3 LOW, no DESIGN-RETHINK FLAG. Report at 00_META/proposals/2026-05-14_plan-01a-audit-report.md. Per Ricardo's decision, the 7 HIGH findings were patched inline into Plan 01a (rather than re-running writing-plans) because the auditor's own recommended path was surgical patches; defects were localized to specific tasks. Patched: Finding 1 (gitignore *secrets* glob → surgical patterns + allowlist), Finding 2 (C1 leak-test passphrase-hang false PASS → poll-based positive+negative control), Finding 3 (secret-scan on-disk vs staged-blob → git show :path + stage-then-edit integration test), Finding 4 (SIGNED_PDF tilde quoting → $HOME), Finding 5 (Task 7 git add after rmdir → git add -A), Finding 6 (C2 reverse-direction underspecified for JP Light-mode → Signal-attachment flow), Finding 7 (run-with-secrets 2>/dev/null swallows age errors → captured-stderr log). MEDIUM/LOW deferred.

tasks.subject: Execute Plan 01a — Foundation (after re-audit)
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-05-16. Tasks 1-11 executed 2026-05-15 via superpowers:subagent-driven-development (16 commits: 10 task + 6 hardening). Tasks 12-18 autonomous parts executed 2026-05-16 via the same skill loop (6 commits): Task 12 verify-only (JP pubkey already on file b7e39bf) / ff01d52 Task 13 Direction A (encrypt sentinel + push, JP-side decrypt deferred) / f63f82b Task 14 (skipped Step 3 decrypt-verify; encrypt-side smoke confirmed 2 X25519 stanzas) / b6bfd70 Task 15 (wrapper + leak test; Steps 4-5 interactive runs deferred) / 5882af1 Task 16 + ed9a596 hardening (&&-chain age+shred in rotation runbook) / acdcc4a Task 17 reframed (markdown is the agreement; PDF encrypt + sensitive_index row skipped per 2026-05-16 directive). v0.1a-foundation tag landed on acdcc4a. F5 closed via alternate satisfaction (markdown per feedback_prefer_architecture_over_ceremony.md). C1 closed. C2 Direction A confirmed at age-parse level (two X25519 stanzas per artifact); full bidirectional decrypt roundtrip + sentinel cleanup deferred to t-plan-01a-jp-and-tty-deferred. Code-quality Minor findings (NOTION row staleness in MANIFEST.md, AGE_ERR not in cleanup trap) tracked for post-Plan-01c polish + bundled into t-spec-notion-removal-amendment scope.

tasks.subject: Plan 01a JP-side + TTY-deferred items (close C2 fully; sentinel cleanup)
tasks.notes: Deferred from Plan 01a 12-18 execution per 2026-05-16 directive (build now; JP downloads on his own schedule). NOT BLOCKING Plan 01b — Plan 01b can execute in parallel; this task can close anytime JP and Ricardo coordinate. Items: (1) Task 13 Step 2 — JP runs own-key roundtrip on jp-mac (encrypts a test sentinel to both recipients, decrypts with his key, confirms 'roundtrip works' via Signal); (2) Task 13 Direction A confirmation — JP downloads vault/keys/sentinel-ricardo-to-jp.age from Gitea web (or git clone if Heavy), decrypts via `age -d -i ~/.config/age/nexostrat.key.age sentinel-ricardo-to-jp.age`, confirms via Signal; (3) Task 13 Direction B — Ricardo Signals the recipients-file plaintext to JP; JP encrypts a sentinel to both recipients via `age -R /tmp/nexostrat-recipients.txt -o /tmp/sentinel-jp-to-ricardo.age`, Signal-attaches; Ricardo saves attachment, decrypts via `age -d -i ~/.config/age/nexostrat.key.age`, confirms back; (4) Task 13 Step 4 — sentinel cleanup commit removing both sentinel files in one `git rm` (gated on Directions A+B both confirmed); (5) ✅ Task 14 Step 3 + secrets.env.age NOTION strip — DONE 2026-05-16 in commit (this strip + MANIFEST update). Decrypt + line-count delta + re-encrypt verified; both X25519 stanzas confirmed in new ciphertext (817 bytes); `grep -rn NOTION_API_KEY infra/` empty post-strip. Closes the ADR-038 follow-on from the hard-system-audit sweep. (6) Task 15 Steps 4-5 — interactive leak test run (positive + negative control); (7) Task 18 Steps 1-3 — full Plan 01a test rerun (gitignore + secret-scan + schema + leak-test) + bidirectional roundtrip + PDF check (PDF skipped per F5 alternate satisfaction). (8) Plan 01b Tasks 2+3 wrapper smoke-test (added 2026-05-16) — run `infra/scripts/run-with-secrets.sh sh -c 'echo "GITHUB len: ${#GITHUB_MIRROR_PAT}; CODEBERG len: ${#CODEBERG_MIRROR_PAT}"'`; both lengths must be non-zero (40 each for the classic PATs landed in commit f1fc501). Gate for Plan 01b Tasks 4-5: must run before the systemd mirror services depend on these env vars in production, otherwise a wrapper regression could result in silent empty-credential pushes. USE DIRECT `-i ~/.config/age/nexostrat.key.age` DECRYPT PATTERN — NOT process-substitution (proven by Task 11 + vault README hardening f747312; tracked broader in t-plan-01a-text-amendments, closed in commit 7e950ee). Closing remaining items 1-4 + 6-8 closes C2 fully + closes audit Finding H5.

tasks.subject: Execute Plan 01b — Mirror cluster (Tasks 1-6 of the 12-task plan)
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-05-16 (20 days ahead of due). Mirror cluster (Tasks 1-6) executed via superpowers:subagent-driven-development in 8 commits between beff92a..d38e865, tagged v0.1b-mirrors-only on d38e865. Original task scope was 'Mirrors + warm-standby' but the warm-standby cluster (Tasks 7-12) is structurally separable and gates on physical second host — split into new task t-plan-01b-execute-warm-standby on 2026-05-16. Commits: 615372a Task 1 (system_map.md + incidents/) → 314ada6 fixup-1 (factual corrections: gitea v1.25.5, 0.0.0.0 binding, real SSH aliases, TS rsync source) → 4dd9301 fixup-2 (realign GitHub mirror to firm namespace nexostrat/nexostrat — reversed the asymmetric-topology detour after verifying via alias that firm key DOES auth as 'nexostrat' on github.com) → f1fc501 Tasks 2+3 bundle (both remotes + both PATs into secrets.env.age in single atomic age cycle + MANIFEST update; F7 closed) → d9cdf3a Tasks 4+5 artifacts (mirror-push.sh + install-systemd-units.sh + 4 systemd unit files + system_map.md systemd table) → 260dc75 pre-install patch (phantom After=gitea.service removed; systemctl enable suppression replaced with systemd-analyze verify + propagated errors; cross-cutting note corrected to reflect SSH-only) → d04191d test sentinel → d38e865 Tasks 4+5+6 post-validation (Mirrors table populated with measured 60s window: GitHub 3s, Codeberg 8s). Closes C4 (Gitea-internal hook decrypt impossibility, replaced by host-side path-watchers, end-to-end proven). Closes F7 (Codeberg mirror present + validated). End-to-end validation recursive: the redundant manual push to GitHub from the harness was BEATEN by the mirror service ('remote rejected, already at d38e865') — proof the wire-up works in production.

tasks.subject: Execute Plan 01b — Warm-standby cluster (Tasks 7-12 of the 12-task plan)
tasks.notes: Split from t-plan-01b-execute on 2026-05-16 after mirror cluster (Tasks 1-6) landed clean at v0.1b-mirrors-only tag. Tasks 7-12 (warm-standby cluster) at 00_META/plans/2026-05-14_plan-01b-mirrors.md lines 1003+. Gates on physical second host (Linux Mint 22.2 + Tailscale-joined). When the host is available: bootstrap (Task 7 inventory + 8 clone + age key recovery roundtrip), populate (Task 9 first rsync), install (Task 10 nexostrat-warm-rsync timer + service), validate (Task 11 systemctl start && grep status=0/SUCCESS), write failover runbook (Task 12 docs/runbooks/hp_down.md). Tag v0.1b-mirrors on Task 12 completion. ~2-3h wall-time. Due 2026-06-30 reflects the physical-host gate; advance once host is in hand.

tasks.subject: Batch 3 step 5 — Re-audit Plan 01c
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-05-16 (20 days ahead of due). 5th audit at this discipline; dispatched general-purpose Opus 4.7 agent with risk-auditor persona inlined (inline brief, no separate file). Sub-agent harness blocked direct .md creation per a recent system reminder; parent agent persisted the report verbatim from sub-agent's final message. Verdict YELLOW (large): 0 CRITICAL, 7 HIGH, 8 MEDIUM, 6 LOW, no DESIGN-RETHINK. Report at 00_META/proposals/2026-05-16_plan-01c-reaudit-report.md (commit f28342a, 384 lines). All 7 HIGH + 8 MEDIUM patched same-session in 3-commit arc: f28342a (report) → cbb5e27 (HIGH H1-H7: warm-rsync SKIP gate + 2 process-sub sites + per-template Architecture/Context + CHECKPOINT cp direction + rule1 restored tighter + TTY-gate leak check + no-commit mirror parity) → 6cb1869 (MEDIUM M1-M8: tag fix bundled with H1 + parens + honest docs-pair error + GEMINI vault constraint inline + mtime MVP note + inliner fixed-point + tag-msg per-plan attribution + jp-heavy.yaml signal removal + Task 9 Signal sweep). LOW (L1-L6) deferred to t-plan-01c-polish-pass per audit recommendation (d). H1 was the dominant defect — without that fix Plan 01c could not have actually tagged v0.1-foundation at the v0.1b-mirrors-only baseline. H4 was a near-miss data-loss bug that would have destroyed CHECKPOINT.md on every Plan 01c execute test run. Mirror cluster recursive validation: 3-commit arc converged to GitHub + Codeberg within ~12 s (second post-tag validation; system works without manual intervention). Unblocks t-plan-01c-execute.

tasks.subject: Regenerate the 2026-05-14 presentation HTML clean from current spec
tasks.notes: Created 2026-05-16 per hard-system-audit Finding H4 recommendation. The 2026-05-14 presentation got an interim patch-in-place commit 3964d00 (16 Notion refs strike-through + banner + ADR-038 popover added), but the audit's preferred path is full regeneration from current spec + ADRs + STATUS — clean, no historical scaffolding. New filename: 2026-05-XX_nexostrat-presentation.html (post-sweep date); old file moved to 00_META/proposals/archive/2026-05-14_nexostrat-presentation.html. Also: regeneration should add ADRs 016 + 018 to the popover database (currently missing); ADR-009 correctly absent (Superseded). Effort: ~1 day matching the 2026-05-14 build (brainstorm + write + iterate); reduces to ~half-day if prompted as 'minimal diff from prior version, applying ADR-038 supersessions only'. Gating: nothing — can run anytime; not blocking Plan 01b.
